steps.title: Find the key signal in the parallel structure
steps.content: The word Similarly at the start of the third sentence signals that Laura Niquay does what Tanya Tagaq did. The first example shows Tagaq incorporated throat singing into electronic music. The opening sentence mentions artists who combine Indigenous music with other styles. The blank requires a verb expressing this same action—bringing two musical styles together into a unified work.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Combining two musical styles together is what the passage illustrates: Tagaq incorporated throat singing into electronic music, and Niquay should do the same with powwow and folk-rock. Blended means to combine or mix, which expresses this fusion perfectly. “Confused,” “replaced,” and “compared” don't work—they imply disorder, substitution, and analysis respectively, not musical combination. Niquay blended the two styles together, just as Tagaq did. That matches choice D — D is correct.
